title: Lepidophorum repandum
taxonomy: Lepidophorum repandum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. This family is commonly known as the heath family. Within the family Ericaceae, Lepidophorum repandum is placed in the subfamily Epacridoideae. The plant is native to Australia and is often found in coastal areas. Lepidophorum repandum is also known to be a part of the genus Lepidophorum, which consists of a small number of species.

intro: Lepidophorum repandum, also known as the 'sour plum', is a type of shrub native to Australia and New Zealand, characterized by its small, white flowers and red or purple berries. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Lepidophorum repandum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects.
